pattern: Splitting on a fixed character and dropping empty pieces is a single pass per word if you use the language's own string-split primitive and filter as you flatten.
hints: Handle one word at a time: splitting that word on the separator gives you zero or more pieces, some of which may be empty.
Drop every empty piece before it goes into the answer -- a separator at the very start or end of a word, or two separators back to back, both produce an empty piece.
Flatten in order: walk the words in the order given, and for each word append its non-empty pieces (in the order they appear) to a single running result list.

edge_inputs.description: several words, each split into multiple pieces
separator at both ends of every word
a word made entirely of separators yields no pieces
single one-character word with no separator present
a maximum-length word made only of the separator
two consecutive separators in the middle create an empty piece that must be dropped
a different punctuation character appears in the word but is not the separator, so it stays literal
using a different separator character from the allowed set

stress.description: many words of random length built from lowercase letters and the allowed punctuation, split on a random allowed separator
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    punct = '.,|$#@'
    sep = rng.choice(punct)
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' + punct
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 20)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(length)))
    return [words, sep]
